title: erica platycodon subsp. maderincola
intro: Erica platycodon subsp. maderincola, also known as Madeiran heath, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Erica platycodon subsp. maderincola include its ability to help alleviate congestion, soothe sore throats, and support the immune system. Erica platycodon subsp. maderincola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
